summary: Reworked the sysdolphin baselib memory-card callback hsd_803A949C to substantially improve matching. The decomp.dev report shows hsd_803A949C improving from 58.78% to 93.40% (+1679 bytes), while the PR title describes a 92% match. The main tactic was replacing a guessed contiguous CardCmdEntry model with a strided command-buffer accessor scheme based on hsd_804D1138 offsets, using the existing CardState layout instead of a local CardStateExt, and adjusting callback signature, field names, control flow, and volatile/global access patterns to better match generated code.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation work for hsd_803A949C, including command-buffer access macros, CardState usage, callback signature change, and control-flow/data-access rewrites.
Updated exported prototype from void hsd_803A949C(s32 arg1) to void hsd_803A949C(s32 chan, s32 arg1).

what_changed: Changed hsd_804D7980 declaration from extern s32 to extern volatile s32, reflecting that the command index/global may need volatile treatment for matching.
Removed the local CardStateExt typedef and switched helpers and hsd_803A949C to use CardState, including named fields such as file_info and digest.

Removed the old CardCmdEntry abstraction and hsd_803A949C_GetEntry helper, replacing them with a compact CardBufEntry type plus CMD_S32(off), CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR(off), and CMD_TYPE macros over hsd_804D1138.
Changed hsd_803A949C signature to take two parameters, s32 chan and s32 arg1, and updated the header prototype accordingly.

Replaced entry->field accesses with command-buffer offset reads, for example CMD_TYPE for the switch discriminator and CMD_S32(0x14), CMD_S32(0x18), CMD_S32(0x1c), CMD_S32(0x20), CMD_S32(0x24), CMD_S32(0x28), and CMD_S32(0x30) for state, slot/index, pointers, IDs, sizes, and other command data.
Adjusted close handling to call CARDClose(&state->file_info) instead of CARDClose(&state->xC).
Reworked read/verify/copy branches to inline separate slot-zero and nonzero block validation paths instead of sharing a later CardBlock check.
Replaced state->x430 accesses with state->digest/CMD_STATE->digest in digest calculation and comparison paths around hsd_803B2B20.
Removed the sector_size local and used state->x8 directly in the affected branches.
In case 13, replaced some CardBlock-struct-based field reads with direct byte indexing into state->x0 plus offsets, then passed state and byte pointers directly to hsd_803AC558.
At function exit, now clears CMD_TYPE to 0 rather than entry->x0, then clears hsd_804D799C and advances volatile hsd_804D7980 modulo 128.
smart_moves: The command-buffer macro approach captures a non-obvious strided layout: CMD_S32(off) treats hsd_804D1138 + off as an array of CardBufEntry and indexes by hsd_804D7980, which is more flexible than a single guessed CardCmdEntry struct.
Using existing CardState fields removed a duplicate local CardStateExt guess and aligned names like file_info and digest with shared project structure knowledge.
Marking hsd_804D7980 volatile is a small but important codegen lever for globals modified around asynchronous CARD callbacks.
The header signature update to include chan keeps the public prototype consistent with Dolphin callback-style APIs even though the diff excerpt does not show chan being used inside the function.
Inlining branch-specific validation for slot 0 versus other slots avoided a shared CardBlock path and likely produced closer assembly around offsets and conditional structure.
Replacing temporary sector_size with state->x8 and using direct command macros throughout reduced locals and shaped register allocation/expression trees for matching.

The diff preserves domain behavior around hsd_803B31CC validation, CARDClose retries, memcpy/memcmp copy/verify behavior, and hsd_803B2B20 digest generation while rearranging C to match compiler output.
decomp_lessons: Do not overcommit to an apparent contiguous command-entry struct when assembly suggests fields are stored in parallel/strided command buffers; offset macros may better model the generated addressing.
Volatile on scheduler/callback globals can materially affect matching, especially when a global index is read repeatedly and updated at the end of an asynchronous handler.
Prefer shared project structs once their fields are known; replacing local anonymous x-field clones with CardState improves both readability and matching experiments.
Callback prototypes should match the underlying library ABI even if some arguments are unused in the current body; here hsd_803A949C gained the leading chan parameter.
For matching, sometimes duplicating validation code inside each branch matches better than factoring a shared post-branch check, even if the refactored C looks cleaner.
Direct byte indexing can be preferable to struct overlays in tight parsing code when the target assembly loads individual bytes at fixed offsets.

Named fields such as file_info and digest are useful anchors when converting guessed offsets, but surrounding command-buffer accesses may still need raw offsets until the true type is better understood.
naming_conventions: Helper macros introduced in all caps: CMD_S32(off), CMD_STATE, CMD_PTR(off), and CMD_TYPE.
The temporary command-buffer element was named CardBufEntry, replacing the older CardCmdEntry model.
Existing shared CardState names were reused where available, notably file_info and digest.
The exported callback prototype now names parameters chan and arg1; chan is evidence of a callback channel argument but remains unused in the shown diff.
Unidentified data fields retain x-offset style names such as x0, x8, x20, x24, x170, x270, x370, x3B0, and x460.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a volatile declaration for hsd_804D7980 to force repeated/global-sensitive access patterns.
Model command arguments with hsd_804D1138 plus byte offsets and an hsd_804D7980 index instead of a flat per-command struct.
Use lvalue macro CMD_TYPE so the same expression can both drive the switch and be cleared at function exit.
Keep command pointer casts explicit through CMD_PTR(off) or casts around CMD_S32(off) to tune pointer/integer expression shape.
Remove the sector_size local and express calculations directly in terms of state->x8.
Duplicate memcmp/memcpy/file-id checks in each branch to match control flow rather than factoring common logic.
Use direct state->x0 byte offsets for some CardBlock fields in case 13, likely to influence load order and avoid struct-temporary codegen.
A short polling-style for loop around state->file_info.fileNo was added before assigning state->x20, suggesting a codegen or observed-behavior match tactic.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported one unmatched-item improvement for GALE01: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94 hsd_803A949C improved from 58.78% to 93.40%, +1679 bytes.
No human review comments or review bodies were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Search for other hsd_3A94 command handlers using hsd_804D1138 to see whether CMD_S32/CMD_TYPE-style strided buffer access can replace guessed command structs elsewhere.
Check declarations/definitions of CardState to confirm all remaining x-offset fields in hsd_803A949C and consider naming file-id, slot-state, and digest-related fields.
Look for callers or callback registrations of hsd_803A949C to confirm the chan,arg1 signature and whether chan corresponds to a CARD channel parameter.
Investigate hsd_804D1148 versus hsd_804D1138 history; this PR removed hsd_804D1148-based entry access in favor of hsd_804D1138 offset access.
Compare generated assembly around the case 13 direct-byte reads to determine when CardBlock overlays hurt matching versus direct indexing.
